> **Rescope 2026-07-20: VERIFIED-CURRENT** — no `.catch` was ever added: both `navigator.credentials.get()` calls (now mojo-auth.js:437 and :490) and the raw `getError()` passthrough (:146-152) are unchanged at HEAD; only minor line drift (GitHub OAuth was added and shares the same leak path). Original archived: moved_to_maestro/High-185-passkey-sign-in-leaks-raw-webauthn-error.md.

# REQ-185 — Passkey sign-in leaks a raw WebAuthn error (with W3C spec link) instead of plain language + a way forward

- **Severity**: High · **Effort**: TBD · **Project**: django-mojo · **Brand**: club-axo (engine-wide) · **Reporter**: Avery · **Submitted**: 2026-07-06 · **Source**: QA-clubaxo-2026-07-06

> **Bug + enhancement.** (Bug) a raw browser WebAuthn error — including a W3C spec link — renders
> verbatim to players on sign-in. (Enhancement) no guidance or recovery action. Engine-wide (shared
> `mojo-auth.js`), so it hits every white-label brand on passkey QR sign-in.

## Plain English
Player taps **Passkey**, scans the QR with a phone that has no passkey for the account (common first
try). Instead of a human message the page shows the browser's raw error verbatim:

```
The operation either timed out or was not allowed. See: https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn-2/#sctn-privacy-considerations-client
```

Reproduced on Android + iOS (accts 414-424-7543, 608-397-2919, 2026-07-06). Enrollment already maps
these errors correctly (club-axo); sign-in does not.

## Root cause (re-verified at HEAD 2026-07-20 — all still present)
1. **No error mapping on the WebAuthn calls.** `loginWithPasskeyDiscoverable()` (fn at :418, call
   at **`mojo-auth.js:437`**) and `loginWithPasskey()` (fn at :469, call at **:490** — was cited
   :469) invoke `navigator.credentials.get(...)` with only `.then()` — no `.catch`. The raw
   `DOMException` propagates.
2. **`getError()` is a naive passthrough** (**:146-152**): `return err.message || …` — the
   browser's raw string, W3C URL included.
3. **Handler renders it verbatim** — `templates/account/login.html:298-302` (was :293-298):
   `m.showMessage(MojoAuth.getError(err) || "Passkey authentication failed.", "error")`; the
   fallback never fires. Google/Apple/**GitHub** (new, ITEM-026) + credentials catches share the
   same passthrough (:279-296).
4. **`showMessage` → `el.textContent`** — `templates/account/auth_base.html:107-110`.

Design constraint: WebAuthn collapses no-passkey / cancelled / timed-out into one `NotAllowedError`
(privacy), so friendly copy must cover all three. The mapping to port exists in enrollment:
`club-axo/src/lib/auth/passkey.ts:199-236` (maps by `error.name`, logs the real error).

## Proposed fix
**P1 — Bug:** add `.catch` on both `credentials.get()` calls mapping `DOMException.name` → plain
copy (port the enrollment map; `NotAllowedError`/`AbortError`/`TimeoutError` → "Passkey sign-in was
cancelled, timed out, or no passkey was found on that device."); `console.error` the real error.
Defense-in-depth: strip `See: http…`/URLs in `getError()`/`showMessage` (also covers the
Google/Apple/GitHub/credentials catches).
**P2 — Enhancement:** recovery action in the passkey error state — route to the existing SMS view
(`btn-go-sms` → `showView("sms")`, login.html:307); optional post-auth "set up a passkey" nudge.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No raw WebAuthn/`DOMException` text or spec URL ever renders in the sign-in message area.
- [ ] `NotAllowedError` catch-all yields one graceful message; real error still logged to console.
- [ ] Both `loginWithPasskeyDiscoverable` and `loginWithPasskey` covered.
- [ ] URL-stripping defense also covers Google/Apple/GitHub/credentials error paths.
- [ ] (P2) passkey error state offers the SMS-code recovery action.
- [ ] Verified on Android + iOS cross-device QR with a no-passkey account.

## Dependencies / Notes
- Ownership: confirm WMX patches `django-mojo` directly vs upstream NativeMojo — decides who builds.
- Recent bouncer churn (ITEM-026 GitHub OAuth, ITEM-034 redirect fix, ITEM-006 SMS UX) touched
  login.html but none addressed this; rebase line refs if more lands.
- `/scope` may split P1 (bug) and P2 (enhancement).
- Evidence: reporter screenshots in `qa/clubaxo/drafts/`.

## Plan

_Scoped 2026-07-24 in django-mojo at HEAD (904d8e12). All workspec line refs re-verified: bare
`navigator.credentials.get()` at `mojo-auth.js:437` and `:490`, raw `getError()` passthrough at
`:146-152`, verbatim render at `login.html:298-303`, `showMessage` → `textContent` at
`auth_base.html:107-111`._

### Objective

No raw WebAuthn/`DOMException` text or URL ever renders on any hosted auth page. Passkey failures
show one friendly message (real error still in the console), and — when the group offers SMS
login — an inline "Sign in with a text code instead" recovery action. The fix is library-level in
`mojo-auth.js`, so custom frontends (club-axo) get mapped rejections too on the next release.

### Scope decisions (resolved)

- **Ownership**: built here in django-mojo (upstream). club-axo/WMX consume via the next release
  (downstream pins released django-mojo). No downstream patching.
- **P1 + P2 together, one build.** P2-as-designed (inline recovery action) is small and rides the
  same files/tests. **Excluded**: the optional post-auth "set up a passkey" nudge — post-LOGIN
  nudging is a separate product decision (post-registration enrollment already exists via
  `passkey_prompt`); file separately if wanted.
- **`registerPasskey()` / `credentials.create()` is included** (small scope addition): the hosted
  enrollment page `passkey_enroll.html:88-98` renders `getError()` verbatim — the identical leak
  class with enrollment-flavored copy. Same one-line wrapper pattern.
- `loginWithPasskey(username)` is called by no hosted template (only `loginWithPasskeyDiscoverable`
  is; verified) but is public library API — it gets the same `.catch` per the AC.
- Google/Apple/GitHub/credentials/magic catches (login.html + auth_base.html's own magic/OAuth
  catches at :202/:217) need no per-site changes — they all flow through `getError()`, which gains
  URL-stripping; `showMessage` gains a render-layer backstop.

### Implementation steps

**1. `mojo/apps/account/static/account/mojo-auth.js`** (keep the file's ES5/`var` style):

a. Near the WebAuthn helpers, add two copy maps + a mapper. WebAuthn collapses
   no-passkey/cancelled/timed-out into one `NotAllowedError` (privacy), so that copy covers all
   three. Exact copy:
   - `WEBAUTHN_LOGIN_COPY`:
     - `NotAllowedError` / `AbortError` / `TimeoutError`: `"Passkey sign-in was cancelled, timed out, or no passkey was found on this device."`
     - `SecurityError`: `"Passkeys can't be used on this connection. Open the site at its normal secure address and try again."`
     - `NotSupportedError`: `"This device or browser doesn't support passkeys."`
     - `_default`: `"Passkey sign-in failed. Please try again or use another sign-in method."`
   - `WEBAUTHN_ENROLL_COPY` = `Object.assign({}, WEBAUTHN_LOGIN_COPY, {...})` overriding:
     - `NotAllowedError` / `AbortError` / `TimeoutError`: `"Passkey setup was cancelled or timed out. You can try again."`
     - `InvalidStateError`: `"This device may already have a passkey for this account."`
     - `_default`: `"Could not add a passkey. Please try again."`
   - `function friendlyWebAuthnError(err, copy)`: `console.error('WebAuthn error:', err)` (wrapped
     in try/catch), build `new Error(copy[err && err.name || ''] || copy._default)`, preserve
     `friendly.name = err.name` when present, set `friendly.cause = err` (plain assignment, not the
     ES2022 constructor option), return it.

b. Wrap **exactly the browser-prompt promise** in all three flows — `.catch` placed BEFORE the
   `.then(function (credential) {...})` so server errors from the begin/complete posts are never
   mapped:
   ```js
   return navigator.credentials.get({ publicKey: publicKey })
       .catch(function (err) { throw friendlyWebAuthnError(err, WEBAUTHN_LOGIN_COPY); })
       .then(function (credential) { /* unchanged */ });
   ```
   Sites: `loginWithPasskeyDiscoverable` (:437), `loginWithPasskey` (:490), and
   `registerPasskey`'s `navigator.credentials.create(...)` (:549) with `WEBAUTHN_ENROLL_COPY`.

c. Add `function stripUrls(msg)` (non-string passthrough): remove `See: <url>` fragments
   (`/\s*\(?See:?\s+https?:\/\/[^\s)]+\)?/gi`), then any bare `https?://` URL, collapse doubled
   whitespace, trim.

d. Rework `getError()` (:146): keep signature/shapes. Order: falsy → `'An error occurred'`;
   string → `stripUrls(s) || 'An error occurred'`; **new** — `err instanceof DOMException`
   (guard `typeof DOMException !== 'undefined'`) → `WEBAUTHN_LOGIN_COPY[err.name] || ._default`
   (backstop for any raw browser credential error that escaped a mapping layer); else extract
   `message/error/errors[0].message` as today → `stripUrls(msg) || 'An error occurred'`.
   (Errors minted by `friendlyWebAuthnError` are plain `Error`s — they take the normal `.message`
   path, no double-mapping.)

e. Export `sanitizeMessage: stripUrls` on the public object (with a short jsdoc: strips URLs/spec
   links from user-facing error text).

**2. `mojo/apps/account/templates/account/auth_base.html`** — `showMessage` (:107-111):
   - New optional third param `action`; backward compatible (all existing 2-arg callers unchanged).
   - Error-type sanitize backstop before rendering:
     `if ((type || "") === "error" && window.MojoAuth && MojoAuth.sanitizeMessage) text = MojoAuth.sanitizeMessage(text) || "Something went wrong. Please try again.";`
     (guarded — `_mat` is deliberately usable even if MojoAuth failed to load; only `error` type is
     stripped so intentional URLs in future info/success copy stay possible).
   - After `el.textContent = text` (which also wipes any previous action button), if
     `action && action.label && action.onClick`: append
     `<button type="button" class="mat-message-action">` with `textContent = action.label` and the
     click listener. `hideMessage` needs no change (`textContent = ""` clears children).

**3. `mojo/apps/account/templates/account/login.html`** — passkey catch (:298-303): build the
   recovery action when the SMS view exists and isn't already active, and pass it through:
   ```js
   var action = null;
   if (views.sms && !views.sms.classList.contains("is-active")) {
       action = { label: "Sign in with a text code instead",
                  onClick: function () { showView("sms"); } };
   }
   m.showMessage(MojoAuth.getError(err) || "Passkey sign-in failed.", "error", action);
   ```
   (`showView("sms")` already calls `m.hideMessage()` — the error clears on switch. Passkey-only
   groups: `views.sms` is undefined → message only, no dead button. Passwordless-primary configs
   where the passkey button lives ON the sms view: `is-active` guard → no self-referential action.)

**4. `mojo/apps/account/static/account/mojo-auth-theme.css`** — after the `.mat-message.is-info`
   block (~:245), add `.mat-message-action`: `display:block; margin-top:.375rem; padding:0;
   background:none; border:none; color:inherit; font:inherit; font-weight:600;
   text-decoration:underline; cursor:pointer;` (inherits the `is-error` danger color).

### Edge cases handled

- Server errors from `passkeyLoginBegin`/`passkeyLoginComplete` (403 method-disabled, 400,
  rate-limit) are NOT swallowed by the mapper — catch placement wraps only the browser prompt.
- A message that is *only* a URL strips to empty → falls back to generic copy, never blank.
- Repeated failures: `showMessage` re-render can't stack duplicate action buttons.
- `friendly.cause`/`console.error` keep the real error for QA (AC: "real error still logged").
- Static files ship with `max-age=86400` — deployed pages pick the JS up within a day of release.

### Testing

New file `tests/test_auth/passkey_error_ux.py` (read `docs/django_developer/testit/Overview.md`
first per testing rules; every assert gets a failure message). This is client-side JS — no JS
runtime in the suite — so regressions are source/render assertions, both established patterns in
`tests/test_auth/login_methods.py` (`_render_login()` helper + package-relative source read; copy
the helper, no new infra). No DB fixtures needed (render with `group=None`), so no cleanup burden.

1. **Every WebAuthn call site is mapped** (fails on old code): for each occurrence of
   `navigator.credentials.get({` and `navigator.credentials.create({` in mojo-auth.js source,
   assert `friendlyWebAuthnError` appears within the following ~200 chars (filter out the
   `typeof`-feature-check occurrences in `isPasskeySupported`/`registerPasskey` preamble by
   matching the `({` call form). Also assert both copy maps' `NotAllowedError` strings are present.
2. **getError sanitizes**: source contains `function stripUrls`, an `instanceof DOMException`
   branch inside `getError`, and the `sanitizeMessage` export.
3. **Rendered login page wires the recovery action**: `_render_login(group=None)` HTML contains
   `Sign in with a text code instead` and `mat-message-action`.
4. **Rendered page carries the showMessage backstop**: HTML contains `sanitizeMessage` (auth_base
   inline script) — covers register/contact/enroll pages too since they extend the same base.
5. **Theme ships the action style**: `mojo-auth-theme.css` contains `.mat-message-action`.

Run: baseline `bin/run_tests --agent` BEFORE any edit (build-baseline rule; read
`var/test_failures.json`), then `bin/run_tests --agent -t test_auth.passkey_error_ux`, then the
default suite again — green-to-green. No schema changes → no `bin/create_testproject`.

Manual QA (AC, not automatable here): Android + iOS cross-device QR with a no-passkey account —
tracked as a checklist item for the reporter after release.

### Documentation

- `docs/django_developer/account/auth_pages.md`: login-methods section (~:280-305) — passkey
  failures render mapped copy + optional SMS recovery action; passkey-enrollment section (~:360) —
  enrollment copy map; note the `showMessage` error-sanitize backstop where the base template's JS
  helpers are described.
- `docs/web_developer/account/auth_pages.md`: passkey flow section — document the rejection
  contract for library consumers (rejects with a plain `Error`: friendly `message`, DOMException
  `name` preserved, original on `.cause` + `console.error`), and `MojoAuth.getError()` /
  `MojoAuth.sanitizeMessage()` URL-stripping.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: `**fix**` entry in the rolling top block (passkey sign-in/enroll no longer leak
  raw WebAuthn errors; SMS recovery action on passkey failure).

### Out of scope / observations for separate items

- Post-auth "set up a passkey" nudge (workspec P2-optional) — excluded, see scope decisions.
- **Pre-existing bug spotted while scoping (not this item)**: on OAuth callback both
  `auth_base.html:207-219` AND `login.html:241-249` detect `?code&state` and each call
  `completeOAuthLogin` — a double POST of a single-use code; the loser's error message renders
  behind the success overlay. Harmless-looking today but racy; worth its own board item.

### Acceptance criteria mapping

- No raw WebAuthn text/URL: steps 1b/1d/2 + tests 1/2/4.
- NotAllowedError single graceful message + console.error: step 1a + test 1.
- Both login fns covered: step 1b (plus `create()` for enrollment).
- URL-strip covers Google/Apple/GitHub/credentials paths: steps 1d/2 (shared `getError` +
  `showMessage` backstop) + test 4.
- SMS recovery action: step 3 + test 3.
- Android/iOS QR verification: manual QA note above.
